string.text: Anna
I could use someone watching my back out there. Want to come along?
Sure, I was getting bored anyway. Lead the way.
You should head back to base. I'll take it from here.
Alright. Good hunting, stalker. You know where to find me.
I could use someone watching my back out there. Want to come along?
I appreciate the offer, but I don't know you well enough. Prove yourself to Duty first, then we'll talk.
Tell me about yourself, Anna.
Not much to tell. I grew up in Kyiv, normal childhood. My father was a stalker - one of the old guard. He'd come home with stories about the Zone, artifacts, mutants. I thought he was crazy.
Go on...
Then one day he didn't come home. They found what was left of him near the Army Warehouses. A chimera got him. I was seventeen. Joined Duty the day I turned eighteen. Been hunting mutants ever since.
That's rough. What keeps you going?

Revenge, at first. Now? It's purpose. The Zone is a disease, and Duty is the cure. Every mutant I put down, every anomaly we contain - that's one less thing that can kill someone's father. Someone's family.
You ever think about leaving?

Sometimes. Late at night, when the emissions howl and the walls shake. But then morning comes, and I remember - there's no one else who'll do this. If not me, who? If not now, when? The Zone doesn't sleep, stalker. Neither can we.
What are you good at in a fight?

I can take a beating. Years of Duty training taught me how to stay standing when others would fall. I'm not the best shot in the Zone, but I don't miss often, and I don't go down easy. You need someone to hold the line while you flank? That's me. Someone to draw fire while you reposition? Also me. I'm the wall between you and whatever wants you dead.
What do you enjoy? When you're not fighting, I mean.

Silence. Real silence, not the dead kind before an ambush. I like cleaning my weapons - there's something meditative about it. A good campfire with decent company. Vodka that doesn't taste like fuel. And sunrises. Every sunrise in the Zone feels earned, you know? Like you stole another day from this place.
And what do you hate?

Freedom fanatics and their "live and let live" garbage. The Zone isn't some playground - it's a wound that needs to be cauterized. I hate stalkers who hunt artifacts while ignoring the mutant nests breeding in their backyard. Cowards who run at the first sign of trouble. And chimeras. I really, really hate chimeras.
Thanks for sharing.
Standalone Companions
Standalone Companions
Anna (Duty)
Enable Anna
Enable or disable Anna as a hireable companion.
Spawn Mode
Choose when and where Anna should spawn.
Disabled
New Game Only (Rostok)
Spawn at Rostok
Spawn at Dead City
Teleport Anna
Teleport Mila
Teleport to Player
Teleport this companion to your current location. If dead, they cannot be summoned.
Mila
I need someone professional. What's your rate?
100,000 rubles. Non-negotiable. I don't work cheap, but I don't die easy either.
Deal. Here's your money.
100,000 rubles. Come back when you can afford me, stalker.
Contract's done. You're dismissed.
Understood. You know where to find me if you need firepower again.
Tell me about yourself, Mila.
You're paying for my gun, not my life story. But fine. Ukrainian Special Forces, Alpha Group. Eight years. Counter-terrorism, hostage rescue, the works they don't put in newspapers.
Go on...

I was good. Too good, maybe. Started noticing things - operations that didn't add up, money flowing the wrong direction, people disappearing who shouldn't. When I asked questions, my commanding officer tried to have me killed. Badly. I'm still here. He's not.
So you came to the Zone?

The Zone doesn't care about your past. No politics, no corruption - just survival and money. Clean, in a way. Out there, everyone's honest about what they want. Kill or be killed. Get rich or die trying. No one pretends to be your friend while sharpening a knife for your back.
Do you miss your old life?

I miss having a purpose beyond money. I miss trusting people. But trust gets you killed - I learned that lesson in blood. Now I have contracts, not causes. It's simpler. You pay, I shoot. No betrayal, no politics, no lies. Just business.
What are you good at in a fight?

I don't miss. Eight years of Spetsnaz training, thousands of rounds downrange - when I pull the trigger, something dies. Long range, short range, doesn't matter. I read combat like a book. Flanking routes, choke points, kill zones - I see them before the first shot fires. You want precision? You want tactical awareness? That's what you're paying for.
What do you enjoy? When you're not working.

Professionalism. I respect people who do their job well, whatever that job is. A clean kill, a well-maintained weapon, a plan that comes together. Good equipment - there's pleasure in quality gear that won't fail you. And solitude. I like being alone with my thoughts. Less complicated that way.
And what do you hate?

Amateurs. People who wave guns around like toys and get others killed with their stupidity. Liars - I've had enough of those for a lifetime. Cheap clients who try to renegotiate after the job's done. And idealists. Duty, Freedom, whatever - they're all the same. Fighting for causes that don't exist while the Zone laughs at them.
I appreciate the honesty.
Mila (Mercenary)
Enable Mila
Enable or disable Mila as a hireable companion.
Spawn Mode
Choose when and where Mila should spawn.
